category: Brew recipe dose mistaken for retail bag size

consistency.notes: All non-null/non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text or embedded variant data. 'coffee.roaster_country' is entailed by 'Portland, Oregon' in the footer, i.e. United States. Boolean fields are supported as follows: 'is_blend' false is supported by the single-origin Kenya product details; 'is_espresso' false is supported by 'Roast: Filter roast' and 'best enjoyed as a filter coffee'; 'is_decaf' false is supported by absence of any decaf indication on a standard coffee product page; 'is_coferment_or_infused' false is supported by the described standard washed processing with no infusion/co-fermentation mentioned.

realism.reasons: This is highly plausible as an html-to-text rendering of a real specialty coffee product page. The structure is exactly what you’d expect: navigation, product title, price, size variants, stock/roast schedule, sensory notes, origin details, descriptive copy, brew recipe, shipping, related products, variant block, and footer. The voice is convincingly specialty-coffee retail—specific but not overwritten, with standard terminology like washing station/factory, AA lot, raised beds, filter roast, and classic Nyeri profile. The origin data is coherent and internally consistent: Kenya/Nyeri/Mathira/Gichathaini Factory/Gikanda FCS with plausible varieties (SL28, SL34, Ruiru 11, Batian), altitude, washed process, and seasonal harvest note. The tasting notes and process description fit a Kenyan washed coffee well. The pricing is believable for a US specialty roaster, and the 250g to 1kg price scaling also feels realistic. The variant data block reads like a reasonable extracted backend/product feed, with IDs, SKU patterns, names, and package weights that match the visible options. Only very minor tells exist, such as the slightly neat/perfect completeness of all fields and the explicit '[default_for_inference]' annotation, which is not something a consumer-facing page would normally expose, but as an embedded extracted data block it does not significantly hurt realism.

label.coffee.producer_or_farm: Gichathaini Factory, part of Gikanda Farmers Cooperative Society
label.coffee.sensory_text:
blackcurrant jam, pink grapefruit, raw sugar

In the cup we find the classic Nyeri profile we wait for every year: vivid blackcurrant sweetness, sparkling pink grapefruit acidity, and a clean raw-sugar finish.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: blackcurrant jam, pink grapefruit, raw sugar
label.coffee.producer_text: This AA lot comes from the Gichathaini Factory in the Mathira division of Nyeri, where smallholder members of Gikanda FCS deliver ripe cherry from the surrounding hillsides. After pulping, the coffee is fermented, washed in channels, and dried slowly on raised beds under shade during the hottest part of the day.
